local_names.malay: Kayu Manis Roti Perancis Lembut dengan Kaya

food_subgroup: Bread and toast products with spread

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for kaya-spread French breadstick; closest parent 'bread food product' (FOODON_03302397) used as anchor.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A000E (Bread and similar bakery products) is the closest defensible parent; no specific code for kaya-topped soft French breadstick exists in the FoodEx2 catalogue.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226559009 'Bread (substance)' is the closest parent; no specific concept for kaya French breadstick exists.

serving.description: 1 serving (approximately 2 breadstick pieces with kaya spread)
serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of Kaya Soft French Breadstick at Toast Box consists of two soft baguette-style pieces generously spread with kaya coconut jam, weighing approximately 100 g in total.

nutrient_source.title: Bread, French or Vienna, toasted — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 18033); supplemented with kaya jam nutrient profile estimated from coconut jam composition (USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods, kaya coconut jam reference)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy entry for French/Vienna bread (NDB 18033) as the base bread component, blended with an estimated kaya spread contribution (approximately 20 g kaya per 100 g serving, based on typical Toast Box kaya toast ratios). Kaya spread composition estimated from USDA Branded Foods coconut jam reference entries. The composite per-100g values reflect a weighted blend: approximately 80% soft French bread and 20% kaya spread. This is an estimation; no direct HPB Singapore Foodo entry for kaya soft French breadstick was identified.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — white bread and baguette-type bread GI reference values
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for kaya soft French breadstick. GI of 72 is adopted from the University of Sydney GI Database value for white French baguette-type bread (GI range 70–75, mean 72). The kaya spread (coconut jam, predominantly sugar and coconut milk) contributes additional simple sugars but does not substantially alter the bread-dominant GI. Glycemic load per 100 g serving calculated as: GL = (GI × carbohydrate_g) / 100 = (72 × 54.0) / 100 = 38.9.

hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size of this breadstick, as the sodium content of approximately 310 mg per 100 g contributes meaningfully toward the recommended daily limit of 2000 mg; pairing with a low-sodium meal is advisable.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index (72) and high carbohydrate load (54 g per 100 g) with added sugar from kaya spread
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should consume this item sparingly, as the high GI of 72 and substantial carbohydrate content (54 g/100 g) combined with the sugar-rich kaya spread will cause a rapid postprandial blood glucose rise; portion control and pairing with protein or fibre sources is strongly recommended.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from butter and kaya spread (2.4 g per 100 g) and moderate sugar content
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals managing cardiovascular disease risk should moderate intake of this breadstick due to its saturated fat content from butter and coconut-based kaya spread, and its high sugar load, both of which may adversely affect lipid profiles and glycaemic control when consumed frequently.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Kaya Soft French Breadstick is a Singaporean café-style toast item consisting of a soft, lightly baked French baguette-style breadstick generously spread with kaya, a traditional Southeast Asian coconut jam made from coconut milk, eggs, sugar, and pandan leaf. It is a signature menu item at Toast Box, a popular Singaporean kopitiam-style café chain. The bread is notably softer and more pillowy than a classic French baguette, designed to absorb the sweet, fragrant kaya spread. The flavour profile is mildly sweet with a distinct pandan-coconut aroma, and the texture is soft and slightly chewy. Nutritionally, the item is carbohydrate-dense (approximately 54 g carbohydrate per 100 g) with a high glycemic index of approximately 72, making it a high-GL food. The kaya spread contributes added sugars and a small amount of saturated fat from coconut milk. Sodium content is moderate at around 310 mg per 100 g. This item is suitable for vegetarians and is typically halal-certified at Toast Box outlets. It is not suitable for individuals with gluten intolerance or egg allergies. Clinically, it is a high-GI, high-carbohydrate food that requires portion control for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease risk. It is commonly consumed as a breakfast or snack item, often paired with soft-boiled eggs and kopi (local coffee).
common_user_queries: How many calories in Toast Box kaya breadstick?
What is kaya spread made of?
Is kaya breadstick suitable for diabetics?
Kaya toast vs kaya breadstick difference

confidence_reason: No direct HPB Singapore Foodo or equivalent primary regional database entry was found for kaya soft French breadstick as a composite dish. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy French/Vienna bread data blended with an estimated kaya spread contribution. GI is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database value for white French baguette-type bread. Confidence is medium due to the adapted/estimated nature of both nutrient and GI data.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are estimated from a weighted composite of USDA SR Legacy French/Vienna bread (NDB 18033, ~80% by weight) and a kaya coconut jam reference (~20% by weight). No direct laboratory analysis or HPB Foodo entry for this specific product was identified. 2. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy bread reference entry used. 3. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted source composite. 4. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted source composite. 5. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0; confirmed zero — bread and kaya spread contain no meaningful vitamin C; this is confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central reference data. 6. GI of 72 is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database white French baguette values; no direct GI measurement for kaya soft French breadstick exists in the literature. 7. Glycemic load per serving calculated as GL = (72 × 54.0) / 100 = 38.9 per 100 g serving. 8. Halal status reflects Toast Box's general halal certification in Singapore; individual outlet certification should be verified. 9. Allergen list includes coconut as a tree nut allergen per Singapore Food Agency allergen labelling guidelines.
